_comment: Committed regression budgets for the LifeOps quality benchmarks (#10723), mirroring recall-bench/budgets.json. The gates compare MEASURED scores against these and fail when a floor/ceiling is crossed; when a run beats a budget with headroom the gate prints it, prompting a ratchet. Both lanes are keyless + deterministic: triage drives the REAL plugin-inbox classifier parse/validate/fail-closed path with a committed fixed-quality mock model; timeliness replays the REAL plugin-personal-assistant scheduled-task tick over two committed DST windows. baseline.json holds the recorded reference run behind these numbers.

triage._comment: Floors sit between the recorded baseline (baseline.json) and the score one additional misclassification would produce, so a single new wrong/dropped row through the parse/validate path trips the gate. minAccuracy is one item under baseline (49/56 → floor above 48/56); minMacroF1 catches cross-class drift a per-class floor can miss.

timeliness._comment: Counts are exact contract values (a missed, duplicated, early, or occurrence-mislabeled fire is a bug, not a tolerance). Deviation ceilings: a 5-minute tick cadence bounds honest lateness at <300000ms; the mean ceiling has ~40% headroom over the recorded baseline mean.
